title: Dublin Cabin Crew Opportunities (Dubai Based, Relocation Provided)
description: **Job Purpose** A personality that shines, the ability to adapt to any situation and make people feel at ease. These are a few of the qualities we’re looking for in our cabin crew. As the face of Emirates, you’ll be the person customers turn to for help and direction when they fly with us, so you need to be friendly, observant and able to provide the right support. Being a member of the cabin crew is so much more than a service role \- safety is our highest priority. You’ll need to lead confidently and take control when it comes to managing aircraft services, security, and safety procedures. This comes from the world\-class learning experience our crew receive at our state\-of\-the\-art facility in Dubai. **Qualifications \& Experience** **Here are some other things we look for in our cabin crew:** * You’ve had more than a year’s experience in hospitality/customer service * You have a positive attitude and the natural ability to provide excellent service in a team environment, dealing with people from many cultures * Your minimum qualification is high school graduate (Grade 12\) * You need to be fluent in written and spoken English * You’re at least 160cm tall and can reach 212 cm while standing on tiptoes, to enable you to reach emergency equipment on all aircraft types * No visible tattoos while you’re in Emirates cabin crew uniform (without covering them with bandages or cosmetics) * As Emirates cabin crew, you’ll be based in Dubai and will need to meet the UAE's employment visa requirements Aside from the requirements of the role, you should be determined to always perform to the highest standards, focus on being solution oriented, and be able to manage a demanding work schedule. You should have the ability to deliver an authentic experience to our customers. You’ll be culturally aware and reflect the Emirates personality \- professional, empathetic, progressive, visionary and cosmopolitan. Before clicking the Apply button, please keep the following **documents** ready to submit with your application: * Recent CV in English * Recent photo **Salary \& Benefits** Your pay is made up of three components: 1. a fixed basic salary, 2. an hourly pay for operated flights and 3. an overseas meal allowance. Basic salary \= AED 4,980 / month, Flying Pay \= AED 69\.60 / hour based on avg. 80\-100 hours / month, Average Total Pay \= AED 11,244 / month (\~USD 3,100, EUR 2,600 or GBP 2,280\) \* These are approximate numbers for Grade II (Economy Class). Meal allowances for night stops are credited to the salary in arrears the following month. Hotel accommodation as well as transport to and from the airport is provided by the company.

title: E-Commerce Assistant
description: We are seeking a highly motivated E\-Commerce assistant to join our head office team. The role supports the day\-to\-day running of the online store, ensuring products, promotions, content, and orders are accurate, up to date, and trading effectively. This role is hands\-on, detail\-focused, and plays a key part in delivering a smooth customer journey and supporting online sales growth. **Key Responsibilities** **Website \& Product Management** * Upload and maintain products on the e\-commerce platform (Shopify). * Ensure product titles, descriptions, images, pricing, barcodes are accurate. * Assist with category management, collections, filters, and navigation. * QA website changes before and after updates or launches. * Conduct regular tests to improve user experience **Promotions \& Trading Support** * Support the setup of promotions, discounts, bundles, and free gifts. * Check promotional pricing, banners, and messaging are correct and live on time. * Assist with campaign launches (e.g. seasonal sales, brand events, BFCM). * Monitor live promotions and report any issues quickly. **Order \& Customer Support** * Support order management and issue resolution in collaboration with customer service and warehouse teams. * Assist with investigating delivery issues, refunds, and returns. * Ensure customer\-facing information (delivery, FAQs, T\&Cs) is accurate and up to date. **Cross\-Team Collaboration** * Work closely with marketing, buying, warehouse, and customer service teams. * Liaise with agencies or third\-party app providers when required. * Support new site features, system upgrades, or process improvements. **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Experience with Shopify or another e\-commerce platform * Good written communication skills * Ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast\-paced environment * Confident using digital tools and learning new systems * Basic understanding of online promotions, discounting, or merchandising * Experience in retail, pharmacy, beauty and health an advantage **Personal Attributes** * Highly organised and process\-driven * Proactive and willing to ask questions * Team player with a positive, can\-do attitude * Interested in e\-commerce, digital, and online retail **What Success Looks Like** * Website content and products are consistently accurate and up to date * Promotions launch smoothly with minimal errors * Issues are spotted early and resolved quickly * The e\-commerce team is well supported in day\-to\-day trading activity

title: EMEA Financial Risk Actuary
description: **Requirements** **Description and Requirements** **The Team You Will Join** The Risk function within MetLife helps the organization to identify, aggregate, measure, report and manage risks across the company. The Europe Risk Function is led by the Chief Risk Officer (‘CRO’), with a Central Team (in Dublin), and local teams operating in the branches across Europe. The central team in Dublin is organized to focus on Solvency support, Risk Analytics, Enterprise Risk Management and Governance, driving consistent risk processes and risk mitigation across Europe and the wider EMEA region and supporting in\-country risk presence. **The Opportunity** The role is primarily focused on supporting the EMEA Risk Management organization in assessing and monitoring key financial risk and solvency exposures in the region, and reporting these to the specific entity risk committees and board committees, regional risk committees and the Global Risk Management (GRM) function and other groups in the U.S. Home Office, and the Central Bank of Ireland as the supervisor for the primary EU based entities or the Prudential Regulatory Authority for the UK based entity. The role holder will report to the AVP Head of Solvency \& Risk Analytics. You will work with key stakeholders across Risk Management, Actuarial, Investments, Reinsurance and Finance. This is a great opportunity for someone to gain exposure both to ALM / investments and risk management in an insurance context. It is a varied and challenging role, with significant potential for professional development, and you will also be expected to actively participate in ongoing projects / initiatives outside of your ‘BAU’ work. **How You’ll Help Us Build a Confident Future (Key Responsibilities)** * * Review, understand and challenge the results of the ORSA/stress testing projections. * Perform analysis of own solvency needs for EMEA entities as required in a consistent manner. * Support the development of Recovery Plans for in scope subsidiaries in the EMEA region. * Contribute to the quarterly consolidated risk reporting on credit risk, subsidiary solvency, economic capital, risk appetite, emerging risks and the ORSA for the various risk committees and board(s); manage appropriate review by senior stakeholders. * Provide standardized monitoring and analysis of financial market trends, risks, and support the AVP Head of Solvency and Risk Analytics to assess impact on solvency and to recommend region\-wide mitigation strategies. * Review and challenge proposals with material risk and capital implications e.g. dividend payments, product developments, new business deals, M\&A transactions. * Support the continued development of the risk policy suite and risk appetite for financial risks. **What You Need To Succeed (Required Qualifications)** * * Recently qualified actuary with 3\+ years financial services experience, preferably in a life insurance company. * Excellent communication skills \- both written and oral with the ability to explain complex subject matters to both technical and non\-technical audiences so that issues are understood quickly and stakeholders are suitably engaged. * Ability to deliver robust analysis with utmost diligence and integrity; keeping appropriate records and documentation **What Can Give You An Edge (Additional Skills)** * CERA * Knowledge of Solvency II framework * Financial\-modelling expertise **Benefits We Offer** Our Ireland benefits address holistic well\-being with programs for physical and mental health, financial wellness, and support for families. We are committed to employee care by delivering comprehensive inclusive offerings and cultivating an environment that drives professional growth, purposeful work, and a supportive culture. Our benefits package includes hybrid work, career development and professional qualification support, full private health insurance for employees and their immediate family members, income protection, defined contribution pension benefits and much more! **About MetLife** Recognized on Fortune magazine's list of the "World's Most Admired Companies" and Fortune World’s 25 Best Workplaces™, MetLife, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, is one of the world’s leading financial services companies; providing insurance, annuities, employee benefits and asset management to individual and institutional customers. With operations in more than 40 markets, we hold leading positions in the United States, Latin America, Asia, Europe, and the Middle East. Our purpose is simple \- to help our colleagues, customers, communities, and the world at large create a more confident future. United by purpose and guided by our core values \- Win Together, Do the Right Thing, Deliver Impact Over Activity, and Think Ahead \- we’re inspired to transform the next century in financial services. At MetLife, it’s \#AllTogetherPossible . Join us!

title: EV Operations Engineer
description: **The Role:** Join a fast\-growing tech company delivering EV charging solutions across Ireland. This role blends hardware, software, and customer support, giving you hands\-on exposure to connected devices and real\-world infrastructure. **Responsibilities:** * Provide **1st and 2nd line operations support** across EV charging hardware and software platforms * Troubleshoot issues related to **hardware, connectivity, networking, and user access** * Manage incoming support tickets via ticketing systems * Monitor live systems and proactively respond to alerts or faults * Support **remote diagnostics** , onboarding, and customer issue resolution * Coordinate with internal engineering teams and third\-party partners to escalate complex issues * Manage and support EV chargers installed at customer homes and commercial sites * Occasionally travel to customer sites to diagnose and resolve technical issues * Maintain accurate documentation and contribute to the internal knowledge base * Take full ownership and accountability for customer issues from start to resolution **Required:** * **Experience** in technical support, IT support, or field support roles * Degree in **Electrical Engineering, IT or a related field** * Strong troubleshooting skills across **hardware and software environments** * Experience using ticketing systems. * Excellent communication skills with a strong **customer\-first mindset** * Professional, outgoing personality with strong interpersonal skills * Knowledge in EV, IoT, telecoms, or energy sector is a plus *** Full, clean driver’s licence**

title: Electrical Engineers – Upskill for the Energy Transmission & Distribution Sector
description: **Work with Us. Change the World.** At AECOM, we're delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world's trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world's most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations. There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of nearly 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world. We're one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us. **Job Description** **Start Here. Grow Here.** **Electrical Engineers – Upskill for the Energy Transmission \& Distribution Sector** Are you an Electrical Engineer looking to move into a new sector? Do you feel limited by your current industry or unsure how to transition your skills into a growing market? At AECOM, we recognise that qualified electrical engineers bring highly transferable technical expertise, problem\-solving capability, and experience working on complex engineering systems. Whether your background is in water, transportation, buildings, manufacturing, consultancy, or another engineering discipline, we can support your move into the Energy Transmission \& Distribution (T\&D) sector. **We are offering a unique development opportunity for Electrical Engineers to join our growing UK \& Ireland T\&D team.** Working alongside experienced industry specialists, you will receive structured mentoring, technical guidance, and access to industry\-leading training to help you build expertise across: * Transmission \& Distribution Systems * Transmission Cables * Substations * Grid Infrastructure * HV Power Systems You will also have access to AECOM University and our extensive internal technical networks to support your professional development. Opportunities are available across our UK \& Ireland offices, \- **Glasgow, Edinburgh, Newcastle, Manchester \& Dublin** — while also benefiting from our flexible hybrid working model. **Why Join AECOM?** **Impact That Matters** Work on nationally significant energy infrastructure projects that support the UK \& Ireland’s transition to a low\-carbon future while improving communities and protecting the environment. **Award\-Winning Excellence** Join an industry\-leading team delivering innovative engineering solutions across major transmission and distribution programmes. **Career Development** Whether you are early in your career or already progressing toward Chartership, you’ll benefit from: * Structured mentoring * Technical practice networks * AECOM University * Leadership development opportunities * Support from experienced T\&D professionals **Energy at AECOM** At AECOM, we are helping lead the transformation of the energy sector toward a more sustainable and inclusive future. As the UK and Europe accelerate the transition to clean energy and net zero carbon targets, we are delivering innovative low\-carbon energy solutions that meet the needs of industry, communities, and the environment. **Transmission \& Distribution (T\&D)** The journey to achieving 2030 net zero targets requires major investment in energy infrastructure, including: * New overhead transmission lines * Underground cable systems * Substations * Battery storage facilities * HVDC and subsea interconnectors AECOM’s Transmission \& Distribution team is supporting the network development and grid modernisation needed to deliver this transition — and you could be part of it. **Come Grow With Us** Our T\&D Engineering team delivers services across the full project lifecycle, including: * High Voltage Power Systems Analysis * Cabling * Substations * Transmission \& Distribution Infrastructure * Protection Systems * SCADA \& Communications * System Balancing \& Compensation * Converter Station Design * HVDC Systems * Interconnectors \& Overseas Links **Working to Modernise the UK’s Energy Infrastructure** AECOM has recently been appointed, in joint venture with Arup, to provide design and consenting services for National Grid’s Great Grid Upgrade programme. This major infrastructure investment will strengthen the UK electricity transmission network, helping connect renewable energy sources — including offshore wind — into the grid by 2030 and beyond. The programme will support: * Grid modernisation * Increased energy resilience * Reduced dependence on fossil fuels * Delivery of the UK’s net zero ambitions **Start Here. Grow Here.** Are you ready to join a highly experienced, growing, industry\-leading Transmission \& Distribution team and develop your career on transformational projects that will help deliver the energy transition? At AECOM, you’ll thrive in an inclusive and forward\-thinking environment where your contribution is valued. And let's not forget about the perks at AECOM. You'll enjoy a range of core and personalised benefits designed to support your future and well\-being, customised to fit your lifestyle. Take advantage of career development opportunities, our flexible hybrid working model to ensure a work\-life balance that suits your lifestyle, technical practice networks, AECOM University, and volunteering days. **Qualifications** **Ready to Push the Limits of What’s Possible?** We are looking for candidates with: * An Electrical or Electronic Engineering qualification such as: + BSc/BEng + MSc/MEng * Demonstrable electrical engineering design and/or consultancy experience in any regulated sector * Strong communication skills with the ability to engage confidently with clients, colleagues, stakeholders, and subcontractors * The ability to explain, discuss, and resolve technical issues effectively while capturing and responding to feedback **Additional Information** **Ready to Make the Move?** Join our world\-class Energy team and help shape the future of Transmission \& Distribution. Apply today. We celebrate diversity, including neurodiversity, and believe it strengthens our teams. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and abilities. If you require reasonable accommodation during the application process, please contact: ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com Interested in learning more about life at AECOM? Follow us on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube to hear employee stories, explore our projects, and discover more about our culture. For further information about the role, please connect with the recruiter on LinkedIn: Anne Marie Flynn \| LinkedIn **About AECOM** AECOM is the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, delivering professional services throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. On projects spanning transportation, buildings, water, new energy and the environment, our public\- and private\-sector clients trust us to solve their most complex challenges. Our teams are driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world through our unrivaled technical and digital expertise, a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion, and a commitment to environmental, social and governance priorities. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm and its Professional Services business had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 2025\. See how we are delivering sustainable legacies for generations to come at aecom.com and @AECOM. **Freedom to Grow in a World of Opportunity** You will have the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. AECOM provides a wide array of compensation, benefits and well\-being programs to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families. We’re the world’s trusted global infrastructure firm, and we’re in this together – your growth and success are ours too. Join us, and you’ll get all the benefits of being a part of a global, publicly traded firm – access to industry\-leading technology and thinking and transformational work with big impact and work flexibility. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in each person’s potential, and we’ll help you reach yours. We are a Disability Confident Employer and will offer an interview to applicants who have a disability or long\-term condition, who meet the minimum/essential criteria for the role. Please let us know using this email address ReasonableAccommodationsUKI@aecom.com if you would like to apply through the Disability Confident Interview Scheme. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

title: Employee Benefits Administrator
description: **Employee Benefits Administrator** This is an excellent opportunity for a highly organised and proactive life and pensions administrator to work closely with the leadership team of a growing financial advisory business. **Job specification** Moneycube is an award\-winning provider of investment and pensions advice in Ireland. We enable individuals and companies to take control of their finances through better use of technology, open pricing, and straight talking about money. We work with some of the most recognised companies in the Irish market and offer customers superior service and products. Three things distinguish our business: we empower our customers, from global tech leaders to switched\-on individuals; we put innovative investment portfolios and ideas into practice; and we offer superior customer experience. We are seeking an employee benefits administrator to join our team. This role is arising due to continued growth in our business. This is a varied and important role within a growing business. The successful candidate will combine strong organisational skills, attention to detail, and professionalism with the ability to manage multiple priorities and maintain a high standard of presentation and communication. **Role Summary:** The successful candidate will join a team of established Financial Advisors. They will be self\-motivated and enthusiastic, will ideally have at least three years’ life and pensions administration experience and will want to join a fast\-moving environment which is informal and fun but does not compromise on quality. This person will play a key role in delivering, maintaining, and enhancing group pension schemes, life and income protection cover, and broader employee benefit offerings. This is a multi\-faceted role that spans client relationship management, administrative operations, proposal development, and invoicing. It’s ideal for someone with strong attention to detail and a desire to grow into a broader client\-facing or advisory position over time. This is an opportunity to join Moneycube at an exciting juncture in its growth, and progress your career with a market leader, with the ability to work with a diverse and experienced team. **Key responsibilities** **Client Administration \& Support** * Day\-to\-day administration of: * Group (PRSA and occupational) pension schemes * Group risk benefits (life cover, income protection) * Wider health, dental, and vision schemes * Onboarding new joiners and supporting member exits/transfers * Scheduling and managing 1\-to\-1 meetings and presentations for advisors * Keeping provider and CRM systems up to date with accurate data * Arranging annual renewals of schemes * Setting up presentations and employee benefit engagement activities with clients **Client Interaction** * Responding to employer and scheme member queries * Supporting client engagement through events, webinars, and Q\&A sessions * Acting as a key point of contact for payroll, HR, and finance teams **Business Development Support** * Assisting with: * Proposal documents for new clients * Client\-facing presentations and onboarding packs * Benchmarking benefit offerings and preparing tailored reports * Participating in pitch preparations and tender responses **Operational Coordination** * Coordinating provider quotes and benefits comparisons * Supporting selection and onboarding of insurers and health partners * Managing invoicing cycles, reconciliations, and tracking per\-member fees * Ensuring compliance with Central Bank regulations and internal policies **Skills \& Experience** The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong organisational capability combined with excellent attention to detail and a proactive approach. * 3\+ years’ experience in a financial services admin role (pensions, insurance, or employee benefits) * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Excellent organisational and follow\-up skills * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and CRM platforms * Attention to detail and ability to multitask across projects * Client\-facing confidence and a commercial mindset * Experience preparing pitch decks or proposals * QFA or APA (Life, Pensions or Savings \& Investments) * Experience with PRSA and/or Group Risk (life cover and PHI) * Familiarity with health insurance or wellbeing/EAP benefits (desirable but not mandatory) **About the successful candidate:** * Takes pride in producing high\-quality work and maintaining strong attention to detail * Is proactive and anticipates what needs to be done rather than waiting for direction * Enjoys helping to create a professional, organised and welcoming office environment * Works well in a collaborative and fast\-moving team * Has a positive, professional approach when supporting colleagues and interacting with clients * Is reliable, discreet and comfortable handling confidential information * Communicates clearly and professionally, both in writing and in person * Is comfortable in working closely with senior leadership and able to exercise sound judgement when handling sensitive information. **Working environment** * 37\.5 hour per week time commitment. * Permanent role based in Dublin with flexibility to work from remote locations from time to time. * Opportunity to grow alongside a rapidly scaling firm committed to learning, innovation, and excellence. * Access to Moneycube technology, marketing and operational infrastructure. * Exposure to leadership, strategy, and cross\-company projects. * Reporting to Head of Corporate. **Benefits Package includes:** * Competitive salary based on experience. * Inclusion in the Moneycube company pension scheme. * Flexible working environment * Death in service life cover. * Opportunities for career development within a growing business * Ongoing learning and training opportunities * Regular team social events and company gatherings * Centrally located in Harcourt Street, Dublin Applications with CV should be sent to corporate@moneycube.ie Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €36,000\.00\-€52,000\.00 per year Application question(s): * How many years have you worked in a financial advisory firm? Experience: * Life \& Pensions admin: 3 years (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Location: * Dublin, County Dublin (required) Work Location: In person

title: Engineer, Cloud Operations
description: **Job Overview** The NOC Engineer will be responsible for monitoring, maintaining, and operating applications, batch processes, network, and infrastructure components. This hands\-on technical role ensures high availability (99\.9%\-99\.99%) and supports timely resolution of incidents or technical escalations to meet established SLAs. The position focuses on delivering operational excellence and reliability within automated cloud environments that power iCIMS products. **The Network Operations Center (NOC), a key part of iCIMS Technical Operations, is dedicated to monitoring applications and infrastructure to deliver an exceptional customer experience. The team ensures optimal performance by validating availability, coordinating cross\-functional event responses, and communicating any customer\-impacting incidents. Additionally, the NOC analyzes key performance indicators (KPIs) to forecast future trends and provide initial recommendations to the engineering team.** **The Engineer, NOC reports to the NOC Manager, will be responsible for maintaining the functionality of applications, batch processes, network, and infrastructure components. This role ensures maximum availability (99\.9%\-99\.99%) and drives timely resolution of incidents or technical escalations to meet established SLAs.** **About Us** When you join iCIMS, you join the team helping global companies transform business and the world through the power of talent. Our customers do amazing things: design rocket ships, create vaccines, deliver consumer goods globally, overnight, with a smile. As the Talent Cloud company, we empower these organizations to attract, engage, hire, and advance the right talent. We’re passionate about helping companies build a diverse, winning workforce and about building our home team. We're dedicated to fostering an inclusive, purpose\-driven, and innovative work environment where everyone belongs **Responsibilities** * Ensure Production Stability: Monitor availability and performance across the entire production environment to maintain optimal operations. * Leverage Monitoring Tools: Track cloud resource utilization and performance metrics to identify trends and potential issues proactively. * Data\-Driven Insights: Generate regular performance reports and recommend enhancements based on detailed analysis. * Incident Management Excellence: Lead the restoration of normal service operations swiftly, including assessment, research, escalation, communication, and resolution management. * Execute Production Changes: Implement necessary changes to support both internal and external customer needs * Operational Support: Provide effective triage and resolution for operational support requests. * Documentation \& Standards: Review and refine SOPs, policies, procedures, and system requirements to ensure accuracy and relevance. * Automation Development: Create and maintain automation scripts using Python and Java to streamline processes and reduce manual effort. * Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Apply IaC practices to improve deployment efficiency, consistency, and scalability. * Comprehensive Documentation: Prepare detailed electronic documentation, including SLAs, performance metrics, installation guides, and implementation guides. * Reduce Manual Work: Identify repetitive tasks and implement automation solutions to eliminate inefficiencies. * Performance Reviews: Participate in monthly metric reviews to support uptime goals of 99\.9%\-99\.99%. * Drive Innovation: Demonstrate passion, initiative, and urgency in seeking innovative solutions and resolving issues effectively. **Qualifications** **Technical Expertise** * 2 to 4 years in administration and production support experience with on\-call responsibilities * 1\+ years of strong Cloud provider experience and demonstrated knowledge * Observability tooling experience **Preferred** **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with AWS / AWS Certifications * Exposure to other cloud technologies like Azure and GCP **EEO Statement** iCIMS is a place where everyone belongs. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities. So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at iCIMS. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. If you’d like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at careers@icims.com.

title: Engineering Resource Coordinator
description: **About The Job** The Engineering Resource Coordinator plays a key operational support role within the Engineering Studio, helping to coordinate employee, resource and development activities across a fast\-paced, project\-based environment. The role works closely with employees, studio leadership, HR and operational stakeholders to support the smooth coordination of people, onboarding, training and resource activities within a rapidly evolving business environment. This role is best suited to someone who enjoys working in a dynamic, collaborative and highly coordinated environment, is comfortable managing changing priorities, and values regular in\-person collaboration as part of day\-to\-day team operations. **About You** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in resource coordination, workforce planning, project coordination, operations support, or a similar role within a fast\-paced and dynamic environment. * Experience working within a matrix or project\-based organisation, where priorities, teams and business demands regularly shift. * Strong organisational and coordination skills, with the ability to manage multiple competing priorities and maintain structure in a fast\-moving environment. * Excellent verbal and written English communication skills, with the ability to build effective working relationships across multiple stakeholders, teams and levels of the organisation. * Comfortable working in a highly collaborative environment with regular in\-office interaction and cross\-functional coordination. * Proactive and solutions\-focused, with strong attention to detail and the ability to work independently within established processes and frameworks. * Comfortable managing a high level of coordination activity, employee interaction and changing operational requirements. * Experience supporting onboarding, training coordination, people operations or employee development activities would be an advantage. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and resource planning systems preferred. * A degree in a related discipline is beneficial but not essential. Relevant experience and capability will also be considered. **Your Key Day\-to\-Day Activities** * Coordinate day\-to\-day resource and employee coordination activities across the Engineering Studio, helping to maintain visibility, organisation and consistency across teams. * Act as a key operational point of contact for employees within the Engineering Studio, supporting communication, coordination and employee queries. * Coordinate talent processes, including performance and reward cycles, ensuring activities are organised, tracked and completed in a timely and consistent manner. * Support onboarding activities for new employees in Ireland and internationally, helping to ensure a smooth and well\-coordinated employee experience. * Coordinate training and development activities for junior engineers, liaising with managers and stakeholders to support learning needs and scheduling requirements. * Maintain accurate employee, resource and coordination records across relevant systems and trackers. * Work closely with studio leadership, HR, Talent Acquisition and operational stakeholders to support changing business and resource requirements. * Support the implementation of people and resource initiatives across the Engineering Studio. * Assist in identifying coordination issues, scheduling conflicts or resource challenges and escalate where appropriate. * Contribute to continuous improvement of coordination, onboarding and people support processes. * Support a collaborative and engaged studio environment through regular in\-person interaction and day\-to\-day coordination with teams across the business. ****Why Join Us?**** At Ethos, we’re dedicated to fostering an environment that encourages both **technical excellence and personal wellbeing** . **We offer:** * Flexible Fridays and a 9\-day fortnight working schedule — enjoy every second Friday off to maintain work–life balance. * A culture that values flexibility, learning, and teamwork, with clear progression pathways and support toward professional accreditation. * Opportunities to work alongside some of the most experienced engineers in the industry, within a forward\-thinking, technology\-driven consultancy. **Benefits:** * Flexible working times * 20 Days holidays up to 25 days * Pension Scheme * Working travel out\-of\-pocket expenses * Travel tax scheme or bike\-to\-work scheme * Regular training/mentoring / further college support * Support for Chartership * Subsidised Health Insurance Allowance * A Professional Subscription Fee paid by the company * Paid Statutory Leaves * Healthcare check\-ups and flu vaccinations * Gym / Fitness membership * Heavily subsidised social committee (5 a side, golf society, climbing club and regular social events) Our vision is to: “ **Be the company people want to work with, and for** ” Our values differentiate and focus us every day to be EPIC \- for our people and our customers.

title: Executive Assistant
description: **About the role** You will play a key role in supporting our Chief People Officer, acting as a trusted partner to enable them to operate at pace and with maximum impact. As a member of the Executive Leadership Team, the CPO operates at the heart of the organisation, and this role will provide critical support in navigating that environment. This is a highly visible position, sitting at the centre of the People function, where you will also provide administrative and coordination support to the broader HR leadership team. You will work closely with other Executive Assistants and senior stakeholders across the business to ensure alignment, seamless coordination, and effective communication at the executive level. You’ll be responsible for ensuring the smooth running of day\-to\-day activities, while also contributing to the organisation and delivery of key People initiatives. This includes managing complex scheduling, preparing high\-quality presentations and materials, coordinating leadership meetings, and supporting the planning and delivery of internal events. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide proactive, day\-to\-day support to the Chief People Officer, ensuring priorities are managed effectively and time is optimised * Manage complex scheduling across multiple time zones, including meetings, travel, and key People and Executive Leadership forums * Coordinate closely with Executive Assistants to the wider Executive Leadership Team to ensure alignment of priorities, meetings, and communications * Support the wider HR leadership team where required with administrative coordination, diary management, and meeting logistics * Prepare high\-quality presentations, reports, and briefing materials for executive and board\-level discussions * Coordinate board and management packs, ensuring accuracy and timely delivery * Support the planning and delivery of internal events, offsites, and leadership sessions * Act as a central point of coordination across HR, tracking progress, managing deadlines, and following up on actions * Monitor project status and produce clear, concise reporting for leadership visibility * Liaise with internal teams and external partners to support operational processes, including vendor management and payments * Act as a key point of contact for senior stakeholders, both internally and externally, handling queries with professionalism and discretion * Identify potential risks, bottlenecks, or delays and work proactively with stakeholders to resolve them * Leverage systems and emerging technologies to improve efficiency, streamline processes, and enhance ways of working **About you** * Proven track record as a successful Executive Assistant in a global organization. * Excellent communication skills, written and verbal at all levels. * Exceptional time\-management/organisational skills, and ability to produce high\-quality work. * A driven and adaptable team player willing to go the extra mile and rise to any challenge. * Ability to work well under pressure and manage multiple priorities. * High levels of professionalism, practices strict confidentiality with all materials, and exercises discretion when interfacing with the business – familiarity with confidential HR matters is a bonus. * Confident in working independently, problem\-solving and developing ideas. * Strong business and commercial acumen. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office with aptitude to learn new software and systems. title: Executive Assistant - Corporate M&A (12-month FTC)
description: ### **Description** Arthur Cox LLP is one of Ireland’s leading law firms. We are an “all\-island” firm with offices in Dublin and Belfast. With 600 legal staff and a total headcount of nearly 900, we provide a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks, and financial institutions and established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. **Department: Corporate M\&A Team** **Location: Dublin (3/4 days onsite)** **Reports to: Practice Support Manager** **Contract Type: Full\-time / 12\-month FTC** **Background:** We are looking for a well\-organised and motivated Executive Assistant to join our dynamicCorporate M\&A Department. This is an excellent opportunity for someone in their legal career to gain hands\-on experience in a fast\-paced and technical area of law. You’ll work closely with your Practice Support Manager, the EA team and Fee Earners within the Corporate M\&A team.### **Key Responsibilities** **Personal Assistant \& Legal Support:*** Provide high\-level administrative assistance. * Speed, responsiveness and efficiency are key for this role. * Supporting partners and associates within the team and supporting EA holiday cover, sick leave. **Travel Coordination:*** Proactively manage complex travel schedules, including flights, accommodations, and itineraries. * Ensure all travel arrangements align with partner preferences. * Prepare travel\-related documentation and meeting materials. * Booking conferences. Maintaining and updating client marketing database (Interaction). * **Expense Management:*** Prepare and submit detailed expense reports in a timely manner. * Track and reconcile expenses against budgets and client billing. Ensure compliance with firm policies and client reimbursement guidelines * **3E Billing \& Time Entry:*** Managing AML requirements for client onboarding, ensuring all procedures are followed. * Running conflict checks and drafting Engagement Letters. * Managing billing processes to include time entry, invoicing, client account balances and follow up on outstanding payments. * Managing and inputting timesheets on a daily basis. * Managing audit/accrual requests for clients. * Liaise with the billing department to review and edit pro formas. Monitor billing cycles and ensure timely invoicing and payment follow\-ups * **Client \& Team Interaction:*** Serve as a point of contact for clients, maintaining professional relationships. * Coordinate with internal teams including marketing, finance, and HR. * Support business development efforts, including CRM updates and client event coordination. ### **Skills Knowledge and Expertise** * Minimum 3 years' experience supporting senior legal professionals. * Proficiency in MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook). * Experience with legal billing software (3E). * Experience with BigHand. * Excellent attention to detail on travel arrangements and coordinating travel itineraries. * Excellent judgment, discretion, and attention to detail. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills. * Excellent organisation skills with the ability to prioritise workload. * Team player, who can also work on their own initiative. * Excellent oral and written communication skills. ### **Benefits** We reward, encourage and support our talented people to reach their full potential offering excellent progression opportunities along with a market leading remuneration package and benefits including:* Competitive salary * 25 days annual leave * Immediate pension scheme * Comprehensive health and wellness benefits including full gym and wellness programme * TaxSaver and Bike to Work scheme * Wide\-ranging opportunities for involvement in internal committees ### **About Arthur Cox** Arthur Cox is one of Ireland’s leading law firms and provides a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks and financial institutions, established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, the firm has led the way in the legal field in Ireland, delivering the best service and support for our clients. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. *Arthur Cox LLP is an equal opportunities employer. We value diversity and encourage applications from all sections of society. Please advise if you have any additional requirements, we will happily assist. Any information disclosed by you in this regard will be kept strictly confidential and shared within the firm only as necessary and with your consent.* *Reasonable accommodations provided for the purposes of your interview or which you may require during your employment will not be taken into account in hiring decisions.*

title: Facilities Coordinator
description: **Job Title** Facilities Coordinator **Job Description Summary** The Facilities Coordinator leads and supports the day\-to\-day operations of Sony Interactive Entertainment’s (SIE) Dublin office, delivering a high\-performing, safe, and seamlessly run workplace environment. This role blends hands\-on facilities coordination with strategic workplace management — owning ticket management, vendor relationships, health and safety compliance, space planning, and employee experience. The ideal candidate is a proactive, detail\-oriented workplace professional who thrives in a fast\-paced environment and is equally comfortable working independently and cross\- functionally. **Job Description** **Core Responsibilities** * Workplace \& Facilities Operations * Lead day\-to\-day facilities operations to ensure a safe, functional, and well\-maintained workplace * Act as the primary workplace contact for SIE employees, fostering an environment * Conduct structured facilities and space readiness walks to proactively identify and resolve deficiencies. * Conduct routine and monthly site inspections to ensure the site meets service quality and operational standards. * Ensure meeting rooms, collaboration spaces, and common meet readiness and presentation standards. * Manage all aspects of health and safety for the Dublin offices, ensuring full compliance with applicable regulations and SIE standards. * Support preventative maintenance program tracking and coordination of vendor services (janitorial, maintenance, specialty trades) * Collaborate with building management, local leadership, and cross\-functional teams to maintain operational efficiency aligned with SIE standards. * Ticket Management * Serve as the primary owner for facilities ticket management at the regional sites. * Monitor incoming requests, assign work orders as appropriate, and ensure timely follow through. * Track open and closed tickets and escalate issues that require additional attention. * Maintain strong service delivery by monitoring SLAs, response times, and completion timelines. * Support reporting on ticket volume, trends, KPIs, and service performance metrics. * Communication and Request Intake * Serve as the primary point of contact for facilities\-related Slack channel communications. * Serve as the primary owner of the facilities email alias, including monitoring, routing, and responding to requests. * Gather and act on employee feedback to implement improvements, sharing insights and larger\-scale opportunities with Workplace leadership. * Maintain clear and professional written communication with employees, vendors, and internal stakeholders. * Act as a go\-to resource for employees, addressing facilities\-related inquiries and requests. * Event \& Workplace Experience Support * Support planning and execution of onsite events and employee programming, including coordination of logistics (catering, A/V, space setup, vendor access). * Ensure event space readiness through setup, walkthroughs, and alignment with Facilities, IT/AV, and vendor partners. * Provide day\-of event support as a visible point of contact, troubleshooting issues to ensure a seamless experience. * Assist with post\-event breakdown, space reset, and continuous improvement based on feedback. * Vendor \& Contract Management * Build and manage strong relationships with external vendors and suppliers, ensuring performance aligns with SLAs in collaboration with the Vendor Enablement team. * Monitor vendor safety performance, conduct regular reviews, and proactively address any issues or gaps. * Provide vendor escorting in accordance with site access and safety procedures. * Support maintenance and renewal of service contracts, including documentation and compliance tracking. * Coordinate service schedules, work status updates, and follow\-up communications with vendors and internal teams. * Financial \& Administrative Support * Create and manage Purchase Orders (POs) in alignment with budget and contract requirements. * Process and track vendor invoices, ensuring accuracy and timely payment. * Partner with Facilities Managers to support monthly accruals, including tracking open POs and outstanding expenses. * Assist in budget tracking, variance reporting, and maintaining accurate financial records. * Support vendor onboarding and ensure compliance with contractual and financial requirements. * Create and maintain clear written documentation related to service requests, work orders, and issue resolution. **Skills And Qualifications** * 5\+ years of experience in facilities management, workplace services, or a closely related field * Experience working with Integrated Facilities Management (IFM) contractors and suppliers and monitoring service standards; solid understanding of compliance requirements. * Strong work ethic with the ability to take initiative and function in a fast\-paced working environment with immediate and changing deadlines. * Strong problem solving and decision\-making skills; strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and time management are a must. * Demonstrated strong and effective verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills; ability to interact comfortably and effectively with managers and employees at all levels within the organization. * Proficiency in facilities management platforms, ticketing systems, and Microsoft Office / Google Workspace tools; comfortable using Slack as a primary communication channel. **KPIs / Success Measures** * Ticket response time and SLA compliance * Timely resolution of facilities and workplace requests * Email alias and Slack channel responsiveness * Vendor performance outcomes and contract compliance * Quality of stakeholder communication and employee satisfaction with workplace services * Accuracy and timeliness of financial administration, including POs, invoices, and accruals * Successful delivery of space planning projects, MAC activities, and workplace enhancements * Health and safety compliance and audit readiness INCO: “Cushman \& Wakefield”

title: Fast Food Cook Full Time

title: Financial Accountant
description: Role: Group Financial Accountant Location: Group Head Office in Fonthill. (Hybrid working available). Reporting to: Group Financial Controller and will also provide support to the Group Chief Financial Officer. Key stakeholders * Group Financial Controller * Group Chief Financial Officer * Clinic Finance Teams * CEO’s of Blackrock Clinic, Hermitage Clinic, Galway Clinic * Group Chief People Office * Group Chief Information Officer Role purpose: The successful candidate will play a key role within areas of the Group Finance function such as Board reporting, governance, systems improvement, and weekly reporting. This role will interact with management at all levels and will offer exposure to a variety of tasks involving financial and management reporting, budgeting, and systems project work. This is an opportunity for an enthusiastic and ambitious qualified accountant to learn and get exposure to many financial aspects of a large business. Key responsibilities: * Support the preparation of monthly group management accounts and contribute to periodic forecasting and variance analysis. * Assist in the preparation of annual budgets for group entities and provide support throughout the end‑to‑end group budgeting process. * Support Group Accounts Payable activities, including supplier reconciliations and payment processing. * Assist with year‑end statutory accounts preparation, audit coordination, and tax compliance requirements. * Provide support on technical accounting queries, financial reporting matters, and ongoing accounting issues across the group. * Take a lead role in the development, implementation, and ongoing enhancement of governance frameworks, financial controls, and group policies as Blackrock Healthcare Group continues to grow. * Coordinate and collate Board reporting submissions from each trading division, working closely with the Group Financial Controller to ensure accuracy, consistency, and timely delivery ahead of Board meetings. * Deliver insightful weekly and monthly financial reporting, highlighting key variances against budget and prior year, to support informed decision‑making by the Board and senior management. * Contribute to group‑wide systems improvement and finance transformation initiatives, ensuring the organisation remains at the forefront of modern financial and business technology. * Coordinate the collation and review of information for full‑year financial forecasting across Blackrock Healthcare Group. * Participate in ad hoc finance projects and systems‑related initiatives as required. * Provide support to Group Procurement activities, including financial analysis and reporting. * Support the wider Group Finance function with administrative and operational finance duties as required. Key competencies: * Demonstrated ability to work independently, using initiative and sound judgement to manage responsibilities effectively. * Proven time‑management skills, with the ability to prioritise workloads and consistently meet tight deadlines. * Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to convey financial information clearly and concisely to stakeholders at all levels of the organisation. * High level of attention to detail, with a strong commitment to delivering accurate work to a consistently high professional standard. * Ability to work collaboratively as part of a team, contributing positively to group objectives and cross‑functional initiatives. * Experience with accounting systems and data analytics tools is desirable. * Flexible and adaptable approach to work, including a willingness to work outside of normal business hours when required to meet business or reporting deadlines. * Experience within the healthcare sector is advantageous but not essential. * The role is primarily based at Group Head Office; however, regular travel to hospitals and other group locations is required. Qualifications and experience: * A recognised professional accounting qualification or working towards qualification. * A third‑level qualification in Accounting, Finance, Business, or a related discipline. * 2–5 years’ relevant post‑graduate experience in a financial accounting or similar role, gained in industry, practice, or a combination of both. * Experience in the preparation of monthly management accounts, including variance analysis and commentary. * Experience using accounting systems and strong Excel skills; exposure to data analytics or reporting tools is advantageous. This job role profile is subject to periodic review and may be updated as needed.

title: Financial Accountant
description: **The Role** We’re looking for a **qualified, hands** ‑ **on accountant** to join our finance. This is a broad role with **end** ‑ **to** ‑ **end ownership** of core finance activities across a multi‑entity group, plus exposure to leadership and Board‑level reporting. You’ll suit this role if you enjoy responsibility, can work independently, and like using financial insight to influence business decisions, not just report on them. This role will report to the Head of Finance over the long term, with an initial period providing maternity leave coverage for that role. We are **open to considering any candidates who would like a flexible/part time working pattern** \[3–5 days per week]. **What You’ll Do** * Own **month** ‑ **end** and management accounts across 4 entities * Deliver clear **variance analysis** and balance sheet control * Review AP, AR and bank postings; support and guide junior team members * Support **budgeting, forecasting** and performance tracking * Prepare monthly management packs and bi‑monthly **Board reports** * Prepare and submit **VAT returns** (Ireland \& UK) * Coordinate year‑end processes with external accountants * Contribute to process improvements and ad‑hoc commercial analysis * Contribute to pricing and profitability strategies **Influence \& Exposure** * This role works closely with senior leadership. You’ll contribute to discussions on performance, risk, cash flow and priorities; translating financial data into insight that supports decision‑making. * To do this well, you’ll need strong judgement, confidence explaining financial implications to non‑finance stakeholders, and a solid grasp of the business behind the numbers. **About You** * Fully qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA / CIMA) * Industry background preferred but not mandatory * Strong experience in month‑end, financial reporting and balance sheet control * Comfortable working independently and reviewing others’ work * Analytical, detail‑focused and commercially minded * Clear communicator with non‑finance stakeholders **Why Strata** **Recognition \& credibility** * Engineering Company of the Year 2025 (Irish Construction Industry Awards). * A culture of excellence backed by ISO 9001, ISO 45001, ISO 14001, ISO 27001 and ISO 19650 accreditations. **Purpose\-led** * B Corp certified and part of a movement focused on high standards of social, environmental and ethical performance. **Development that’s not lip service** * Structured performance and continuous professional development planning. * Support for ongoing learning and continued education. **Competitive compensation package** * Competitive salary with annual reviews. * Pension scheme with matching contributions. **Benefits \& flexibility** * 25 days annual leave (plus bank holidays). * Paid maternity, paternity and adoptive leave . * Hybrid and flexible working options. * Wellbeing supports including Wellchoice wellness benefit and EAP. * Death in service cover. * Taxsaver commuter tickets, bike to work scheme. * Culture and social initiatives. **Equal Opportunity \& Inclusion** At Strata, we’re committed to building an inclusive workplace where different perspectives are valued and people feel supported to do their best work. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. If you need any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, or would like to discuss flexible working options, please let us know \- we’re happy to support.
