title: Internal Control Specialist
description: **Internal Controls Specialist \| Dublin \| Consumer Goods \| Hybrid working** Looking to grow your career in risk within a global consumer goods business? This is an exciting opportunity to join as an Internal Controls Specialist, supporting a fast\-paced international operation from its Dublin headquarters. You’ll join a collaborative risk team with broad exposure across financial, operational and regulatory areas, working closely with stakeholders across the business while building valuable long\-term career prospects. **What you’ll be doing:** • Supporting control assessments, reporting and risk management processes • Maintaining risk registers, dashboards and data analysis • Tracking incidents, actions and mitigation plans • Working with stakeholders across multiple business functions • Producing insights to support business decision making **What we’re looking for:** • 3 to 5 years’ experience in risk, audit, compliance or process improvement roles • Strong analytical and reporting skills • Experience using spreadsheets, Power BI, Tableau or similar tools • Understanding of risk management frameworks and concepts • Confident communication skills with a proactive mindset You’ll benefit from hybrid flexibility, strong career development, exposure to senior stakeholders and the chance to join a business known for its energy, growth and people\-focused culture. **Apply today to find out more about this opportunity and where it could take your career.**

title: Investment Analyst - Global Fund
description: **Job Title: Investment Analyst** **Sector: Global Investment Firm** **Location: Dublin \- Hybrid** **Salary: Base negotiable plus exceptional bonus opportunity and benefits** **Our Client** Our client are a Global Investment Firm. They have a track\-record of delivering exceptional results and have an excellent reputation globally. **Why should you apply?** This is an excellent opportunity for a driven and ambitious individual who is seeking to advance their career in an exciting industry. You will be part of an investment team that has exposure to all projects, large scale, niche, and highly specialised ventures. The role will potentially enable the successful candidate to put themselves on a path to a seat at the top table. **Who should apply?** Candidates with prior experience in finance with the following: * 2\-5 years’ experience in accounting, investment banking or structured finance role * 3rd level degree in accounting, economics, finance, actuary, or another numeric discipline * Excellent academics and proven track\-record of high achievement * Ability to analyse large data sets * Highly motivated to take on responsibilities * Post\-grad qualification in finance advantageous but not essential * Concise reporting writing ability * High level of proficiency in financial modelling * Strong attention to detail in both written and numerical data/documentation * Keen interest in financial markets **Role and Reporting Lines** Reporting directly to the Investment Manager works closely with other members of the Investment team, duties will include: * Financial modelling * Co\-ordinating due diligence and carrying out detailed market research * Creating and writing key reports for internal and external stakeholders * Assisting members of the wider team with ad\-hoc presentations and analysis * Reviewing equity assumptions * Stress testing and sensitivity analysis for all potential investments **Interested in this position?** To apply, please submit your CV to Aonghus Cody who is managing this assignment via the link below or to inquire further please contact Aonghus directly at 01 529 4200\.

title: Investor Services AML Officer
description: **Who we are looking for** * The role holder will be responsible for the supervision of the Transfer Agency AML team case managers (A1’s, A2’s \& Senior Associates), management of the AML process for all client relationships and acting as a point of escalation for the AML Team members and TA teams on any AML related queries or issues. They will ensure that policies and procedures are followed and that best practices are applied across the group. They will continuously look for enhancements to create greater efficiencies and productivity within the AML team **Why this role is important to us** The team you will be joining is a part of State Street Global Services (SSGS). SSGS gives asset owners and managers access to the essential financial tools they need to deliver effective investment solutions. From core custody, accounting, fund administration and shareholder recordkeeping, to complete operations solutions and servicing for alternative assets like OTC derivatives, private equity and real estate, SSGS’ tools help our clients make better investment choices and act on growth opportunities. Join us if making your mark in the financial services industry from day one is a challenge you are up for. **What you will be responsible for** As Investor Services AML Officer you will * Be responsible for the setting and achievement of specific Team performance targets and objectives to any direct reports. * Build and maintain client relationships through regular meetings, forums, ensuring effective communication and client satisfaction, and act as a point of contact for client escalation. * Develop and implement key improvements on the AML process, procedures and new policy changes into the department. * Work with internal development on system enhancements to improve AML efficiency and productivity within the team. * Promote a risk awareness culture within the team to drive the zero error policy. * Proactively identify gaps in internal key controls and work with wider TA manager/director group to close gaps. * Demonstrate awareness with regulatory (and internal policy) obligations regarding Suspicious Activity Reporting. * Responsible for the effective management of the team and all associated HR Policies, performance and career developments. * Participate in department wide training efforts – draft and deliver training course and facilitate ‘on the job’ training. * Support business growth through the on boarding of new clients. * Support AVP/VP to engage, develop and retain employees by creating an environment of continued learning and development. **What we value** These skills will help you succeed in this role * 4 \-6 years Fund Administration/ AML experience (preferable Hedge Transfer Agency) with at least 2 years people management experience. * Proficient in Microsoft Office applications in particular Excel. * Experience in working to tight deadlines, with an ability to multi task and to work calmly under pressure. * Experience in Client Relationship Management is essential for this role. * Good working knowledge of AML and regulations impacting the industry. **Education \& Preferred Qualifications** * Degree in Finance and/or Business Related area (minimum 2\.2\) or relevant working experience. **About State Street** ====================== Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers Read our CEO Statement

title: Irish law firm | Real Estate role | Dublin
description: We’re working with a **leading Irish law firm** seeking a **Real Estate Associate (2–6 years’ PQE)** to join its highly regarded Dublin team. This is an excellent opportunity to join a **top\-tier practice with strong cross\-border capability** , advising a broad mix of domestic and international clients. **The Role** You’ll work on a wide range of high\-quality matters including: * Acquisition and disposal of **commercial real estate assets** * Portfolio transactions and large\-scale developments * Advising investors and developers on **PRS/BTR and multi\-family schemes** * Ongoing advisory work on **landlord \& tenant matters** * Collaborating with **finance, tax, corporate, and litigation teams** on complex transactions **What’s on Offer** * Market\-leading salary and bonus structure * Hybrid working * Strong career progression opportunities * Comprehensive benefits package including pension, healthcare and wellness initiatives If interested, please contact hamishrichmond@taylorroot.com

title: Join the Jentic Talent Community
description: **Description** **About Jentic** At Jentic, we’re on a mission to build the core integration layer for AI, enabling developers to connect their AI systems to the world’s APIs easily and securely. We’re creating a critical missing piece of the future AI tech stack — middleware designed from the ground up for the AI era. We’re not just building an AI\-facing product, but an AI\-native company. We’re building a small highly\-productive team, in which everyone is amplified with AI, and where everyone shares personal and financial ownership in our mission. Joining Jentic means shaping the future of software in a team that is built for the future. **What We’re Building** Jentic is the standalone integration layer that connects AI agents with other systems and each other. Built by developers, for developers, our platform simplifies and accelerates API integration, increasing AI agent reliability, while solving security and access control challenges. **Requirements** **About Our Jentic Talent Community** Thank you for your interest in Jentic. We may not have a position right now that currently matches to your experience and expertise, however we would love if you shared your CV with us so we can get in touch with you about future roles when the time is right. Please note that being part of our Jentic Talent Community does not mean that you are actively applying for a role with us. It means that you'll be in our talent list so that when the right role comes up, we are able to contact you. **Benefits** **Why Join Us Now?** * Be a pioneer: Join an exceptionally experienced founding team at a critical stage in Jentic’s journey. Early team members will play a foundational role in the company’s growth and direction. * Mark your mark: Help define the core architecture of the future AI stack and make your mark during this unprecedented platform shift to AI. * Developer\-focused mission: Build tools that solve real\-world problems developers face when integrating AI agents. * AI\-native culture: Learn how to leverage AI to multiply your effectiveness and help create an organisation that reflects what businesses will look like in the years to come. **Take a First Class Ticket on the AI Train** This is your chance to work on a critical missing piece of the future of AI in a startup led by seasoned founders with a track record of success. Join us in building the essential middleware for AI, and help shape the future of software during the most transformative shift in decades. Ready to get on board? Apply now and let’s build the future together. **Equal Opportunity Employer Statement** Jentic is an equal opportunity employer, and we value diversity in the workplace. We are committed to providing an inclusive environment for all employees and candidates. We don't just tolerate differences—we celebrate them. Our strength comes from perspectives that challenge the status quo.

title: Junior Buyer
description: **Position:** Junior Buyer **Division:** Procurement **Reports To:** Procurement Manager **Date Prepared:** April 2025 **Role Description:** Responsible for the day\-to\-day of the Junior Buyer of the Procurement Department by supporting the wider team in all procurement functions along with sole responsibility for the ordering \& overall management of the workwear/PPE orders \& distribution. **Key Accountabilities:** * Generating, processing \& amending purchase orders. * Draft purchase orders and issue to the relevant stakeholders. * Monitor stock levels of office supplies to include but not limited to stationery and consumables. * Manage the procurement storeroom ensuring that it is kept in a presentable manner and all stock is recorded accordingly. * First point of contact for collection of materials/assets by site personnel. * Assist the procurement team in asset tagging all tools \& equipment to include asset transfers. * Support the procurement \& fleet co\-ordinator as and when required. * Support the wider team ensuring that all suppliers have the appropriate accreditations and insurances cover. * Liaise with all facilities \& maintenance teams to include key account managers as required. * Manage internal requisitions and request multiple quotes to ensure the best price. * Monitor supplier performance and internal stakeholders and escalate issues and concerns as they arise. * Forecast price and market trends while identifying key changes that may impact on purchasing and give feedback to the Procurement Manager for consideration \& further analysis. * Identify cost reduction opportunities to achieve the departments financial objectives. * Keep up to date on market trends and innovations, regulations and new technology which may have an impact on how the business will function in the future. * Identify potential areas of improvement based on observations and make recommendations to the Procurement Manager. * Responsible for the day\-to\-day operations of the procurement department to include the shared inbox management inclusive of the shared management of the invoice register. * Understand the range and depth of the services provided by Sensori FM with the eagerness to further develop your knowledge of the business and the services we provide. * Support the Company in promoting a culture of excellent customer service and establish exceptional working relationships with all key stakeholders. * Act as a brand ambassador and lead the Company through its ongoing expansion. * Any other duties as deemed necessary for the efficiency of the business **Critical Competencies:** * Demonstrate the ability to manage procurement \& support services provided to Amazon Account. * Demonstrate the ability to manage Workwear \& PPE stock. * Ability to work in a proactive and reactive manner. * Ability to multitask \& prioritise workload. * Ability to work under pressure. * Demonstrate tangible business improvements. * Compliance with Company policies \& procedures **Qualifications, Experience and Knowledge:** * Preferably has an Irish Institute of Purchasing \& Materials Management (IIPMM) degree or equivalent qualification. * 1\+ years' experience in an administration role. * Proficient IT skills and working knowledge /use of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel etc). * Solid commercial \& financial awareness. * Excellent analytical \& planning ability with exceptional numeracy skills. * Strong negotiation skills. * Positive \& professional manner. * Conscientious with a strong attention to detail. * Organised with the ability to meet strict deadlines.

title: Junior Electrical Engineer/Project Co-ordinator
description: **Junior Electrical Engineer** **RGN Electrical \& Communications Ltd** Location: Naas / Sallins, Co. Kildare Full\-Time \| Office Based Monday to Friday, 8:00am – 4:30pm RGN Electrical \& Communications Ltd are looking for a motivated Junior Electrical Engineer to join our growing office team in Naas, Co. Kildare. This is a great opportunity for a graduate or junior engineer to gain hands\-on experience within a busy and growing electrical contracting company. Key Responsibilities * Assist with project coordination and technical documentation * Prepare RFIs, technical queries, and submittals * Support project drawings and CAD/PDF mark\-ups * Assist with project close\-out documentation and O\&M manuals * Support tendering, estimating, and project administration * Carry out occasional site visits and surveys * Liaise with project teams, subcontractors, and clients Requirements * Degree in Electrical Engineering * 1\+ years’ experience desirable * Good IT skills including AutoCAD and Microsoft Office * Strong communication and organisational skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Fluent English essential * Must be eligible to work in Ireland What We Offer * Competitive salary * Company pension scheme * On\-site parking available * Walking distance from Sallins Train Station * Career progression opportunities * Supportive working environment How to Apply Please send your CV to Recruitment@rgn.ie. Job Type: Full\-time Work Location: In person

title: Junior Graphic Designer
description: We have an exciting opportunity for a Graphic Designer to join our expanding design studio Stray Dog, which is part of the Doggett Group, a leading Print Management Company in Harold’s Cross, Dublin 12\. This role is varied, from design layout to pre\-press, so an understanding of digital and pre\-press file setup is essential. Candidates must have a minimum of 1\+ years’ experience working in a Print/Design background. Key requirements: * Must have good experience with working on Annual Reports and be able to show this in your portfolio work. * Highly Proficient in Adobe Creative Suite primarily: InDesign, Photoshop, and Illustrator is a must requirement for this position. * Ability to take direction well, interpret the brief, while at the same time, applying your own design style. * Professional, creative design ability, speed, flair, good communication and ability to demonstrate a strong work ethic, have excellent attention to detail and committed to seeing tasks through to completion and willingness to observe and learn. * The ability to react with appropriate urgency to situations and events that require a quick response or turnaround. * Verbal and written fluency in English is a must. * Video / Animation / Illustration work is an advantage. * Experience with data merge/mailing campaigns and packaging/carton work is an advantage. Please apply with CV and cover letter outlining why you are suitable for the role plus PDF portfolio or link to online portfolio. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €30,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * On\-site parking * Wellness program Application question(s): * Do you have experience designing reports? Experience: * Graphic design for print: 1 year (required) Work Location: In person

title: Junior HR Business Partner
description: **Company:** ------------ Mercer **Description:** ---------------- **We are seeking a talented individual to join our Human Resource team at Marsh in Ireland.This is a hybrid role that has a requirement of working at least three days a week in the office.** Reporting to the Lead HR Manager, the successful candidate will be responsible for the provision of comprehensive Human Resource services in a successful and growing organization. As a **Junior HR Business Partner,** you will play an important role within the People Function and will support our businesses across Ireland. In this role, you will coach, support and advise People Managers based in Ireland, aiming to develop and enhance the quality of people management throughout the colleague lifecycle; this role can also include project work. You will have the opportunity to advise and support on people matters in a business where the People function is considered an essential partner. The Junior HR Business Partner will also be key to the supporting the deployment and adoption of new processes or initiatives which are aimed at People Managers. Youwill need to understand and reflect the local market perspective whilst respecting the value of enterprise\-wide alignment where appropriate. **We will count on you to:** * Support the deployment of enterprise\-wide HR programs and initiatives. * Provide guidance and support to Managers across the full range of activities in the HR portfolio, including and not limited to performance management, skills and competence development and learning, succession planning, absent/leave management, change management, organizational effectiveness, colleague engagement, compensation, and total rewards including the year\-end process. * Assist in employee relations matters such as performance improvement processes, disciplinary actions, grievances, and appeals, working closely with more experienced colleagues and the Employee Relations CoE for guidance on complex cases. * Supporting the rollout and delivery of HR programs and contributing to process improvements. * Cultivate strong relationships with functional teams (Finance, Total Rewards, Talent Acquisition and Benefits) and support the alignment between the people and business strategies. * Support People Managers and colleagues during organizational changes by assisting with coordination of activities and consultations, working alongside senior HR colleagues to ensure smooth transitions. * Consult and partner with the wider HR network and CoEs to determine best practices, continuously improve delivery methods, and especially consider country\-specific nuances to guide local implementation. * Work with the HR Team and Centres of Expertise to understand and support compliance with regulatory and statutory requirements, contributing to reporting and related activities as part of your development. **What you will need to have:** * 2\+ years of relevant work experience in HR alongside a requirement for relevant qualifications. * Able to operate without supervision for straightforward matters and able to judge when guidance is required for more complex situations and seek support from appropriate colleagues/experts * Contribute to the successful implementation of HR projects and initiatives by supporting project activities and collaborating with stakeholders, gaining experience in aligning HR efforts with business objectives. * Experience in supporting managers with employee relations cases, with a knowledge of relevant Irish legislation. * Able to support People Managers in specific groups within the country on all aspects of the employee life cycle in all typical situations. * Able to identify opportunities to make improvements to the service provided by People Services, and demonstrates a willingness and aptitude to embrace changes that will deliver a better service to People Managers * Effective communications and change management skills. * Attention to detail with high personal work standards. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment, with shifting priorities and a complex matrix organization. **What makes you stand out?** * Bachelors’ degree * CIPD qualified or HR related certifications * Previous experience in the financial services sector, or experience in a regulated company * Familiarity with HR system, like Workday or similar systems, * Demonstrated passion for regular self\-investment to maintain up\-to\-date professional knowledge and competence and enthusiasm for learning new skills like Artificial intelligence **Why join our team:** * We’re looking for someone with a great attitude and a collaborative spirit, eager to join an exciting, dynamic team that thrives on flexibility and adaptability to support a wide range of stakeholders and priorities—an excellent opportunity for someone passionate about learning and growing their HR career. * To be part of a company with a strong brand, committed to making a difference in people's lives. * We help you be your best through professional development opportunities, interesting work and supportive leaders. * We foster a vibrant and inclusive culture where you can work with talented colleagues to create new solutions and have impact for colleagues, clients and communities. * Our scale enables us to provide a range of career opportunities, as well as benefits and rewards to enhance your well\-being. * Marsh is a Certified 'Great Place to Work', June 2025 \- June 2026, Best Workplaces and Best Workplaces for Women. Marsh (NYSE: MRSH) is a global leader in risk, reinsurance and capital, people and investments, and management consulting, advising clients in 130 countries. With annual revenue of over $27 billion and more than 95,000 colleagues, Marsh helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective. For more information, visit corporate.marsh.com, or follow us on LinkedIn and X. ### **Marsh is committed to embracing a diverse, inclusive and flexible work environment. We aim to attract and retain the best people and embrace diversity of age, background, disability, ethnic origin, family duties, gender orientation or expression, marital status, nationality, parental status, personal or social status, political affiliation, race, religion and beliefs, sex/gender, sexual orientation or expression, skin color, Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We are an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to providing reasonable support to any candidate with a disability/health condition to allow them to fully participate in the recruitment process. We welcome candidates to contact us at reasonableaccommodations@mmc.com to discuss any specific needs.** ### **Marsh is committed to hybrid work, which includes the flexibility of working remotely and the collaboration, connections and professional development benefits of working together in the office. All Marsh colleagues are expected to be in their local office or working onsite with clients at least three days per week. Office\-based teams will identify at least one “anchor day” per week on which their full team will be together in person.**

title: Junior Lighting Design
description: **About the Company** The LED Group, an Irish owned company, has a global presence and a reputation worldwide for first\-class customer service and products. LED Group currently employs over 200 people worldwide. LED Group has ISO9001:2008 Quality Assurance Accreditation and insists upon uncompromising attention to detail and care throughout all processes and practices within the Group. The ROBUS range of products integrates new technologies with an emphasis on high\-performance LEDs, focusing on energy savings and functionality. LED Group’s core values are that of accountability – we always do what we say we will do, ambition, empowerment, customer focus and fast pace. As a result of increased customer demand for lighting designs coupled with strong sales growth, an opportunity has arisen for an in\-house Lighting Designer role within the Lighting Design Department at LED Group in the Dublin office. The right candidate will perform a pivotal role in supporting the Sales Team to win projects by providing top\-class lighting designs and quick turnaround times. This is a junior role, full training and support will be given to the successful applicant. The ideal candidate would be enthusiastic, focused, and energetic with a huge appetite to learn. This role is based in our head office. **Role Overview** Lighting Design Engineer supporting sales and project delivery by creating CAD\-based lighting designs, managing multiple projects, and providing technical and tendering support using tools like Relux or Dialux. **Responsibilities** * Working with the Sales Team primarily, and other key stakeholders in the delivery of lighting design projects. * Review and interpret CAD files to create lighting designs that meet specification requirements. * Deliver lighting designs as requested by the Sales Team within agreed timelines. * Project Management, take ownership of lighting design projects allocated. * Support the Sales team in the tendering process. * Carry out a comparative analysis of ROBUS vs Competitor products through lighting designs and reports. * Assist in technical support functions as business needs demand. **Required Skills** * Basic level of Lighting design experience using Relux or Dialux. * Level 8 Technical Graduate in Engineering or Design. * Experience in working with CAD. * A self\-starter with excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to work well in a team environment as well as being able to work individually. * Results focused, the ability to handle large volumes of data and to run multiple projects concurrently. * Persistent and diligent with a focus on attention to detail. * Assertive and dynamic with a willingness to learn and take on responsibility. **Preferred Skills** * Relevant experience in the lighting industry. * Completion of College modules linked to Lighting Design. * Understanding of building services. * Knowledge of BIM is preferred. * Proven track record of office and industrial lighting design. * Experience in working with CAD files to create suitable lighting designs. * Good working knowledge of lighting\-related standards, schemes, and calculations. **Benefits** * 22 days holidays * Subsidised canteen on site. * Pension scheme available (after 6 months). * Company laptop. * Bike to Work Scheme. * Social Activities. * Personal ADR training.

title: Junior Product Development Engineer
description: The successful candidate will join the R\&D team to take responsibility for complex LV distribution projects in the electrical power industry. With excellent people skills, you have a proven ability to work in partnership at all levels. With high levels of commitment, your responsibilities will include leading various stages of projects from concept to completion. This is an opportunity to be involved in a dynamic engineering department with exposure to multiple projects running simultaneously. You will work closely with customers, listening to their needs and implementing innovative solutions using latest technologies. You will be required to commit considerable effort to truly understand our customer’s systems and processes and identify ways to add value. **Responsibilities will include:** * Reporting to the R\&D Manager, the Electrical Design Engineer will work as part of the ECAD team, ensuring deliverables required by the project are developed in line with company protocols and procedures. * Design of critical power distribution systems, PLC based control panels and energy * monitoring systems. * Assemble document packages and develop drawing sets for review * Application of relevant international design standards to all design projects. (e.g IEC * 61439, UL 891\) * Communicate progress on design plan and design deliverables to the R\&D Manager * Submitting designs and BOM’s for review and any other design deliverable documentation for review approval * Coordinating with the assigned project lead, ensuring alignment on deliverables and timelines and any running changes during the design phase of a project. * Supporting continuous improvement \& development of the R\&D Team processes and Controls **Requirements:** * Be degree qualified in Electrical Engineering or similar with minimum 3 years experience in similar environment. * Previous experience designing low voltage power distribution products. * Demonstrated success working to prescribed standards. * Experience with Solid Works Electrical or similar electrical design software. * Experience of Building Management Systems would be advantageous, but not essential. * Have excellent communication skills with a professional approach to work and sharp eye for detail. * Good team player, but capable of working independently. * Driving License and Access to a vehicle * Willingness to travel if required. **Why Hanley Energy:** * **Launchpad for Your Career:** Joining Hanley Energy means stepping into a world of endless opportunities. Gain hands\-on experience that will set the stage for a prosperous career. * **Nurturing Environment:** You won’t just be a part of a team; you’ll be part of a supportive family where your growth is celebrated and nurtured. * **Shape Our Future:** At Hanley Energy, your voice matters. Contribute your ideas and be a driving force behind our continued growth and success. * **Competitive Package:** Enjoy a well\-deserved competitive salary and benefits package that acknowledges your dedication and hard work. **Company Benefits:** * Salary with excellent career development opportunities * Company Healthcare Plan * Company Pension Scheme * Death in Service Life Insurance * Employee Assistance Programme \& Wellbeing Programme * Academic assistance \& Structured In\-house training * Hanley Energy Affinity Scheme * Free parking at all locations * Hybrid Work Model where applicable **Work Location:** Dundalk

title: Junior Project Coordinator
description: **Job Summary** Our client is a well\-established commercial interiors and fit\-out specialist delivering workplace, office refurbishment, and interior fit\-out projects across Ireland. The business supports clients from design and planning through to project delivery, providing turnkey solutions across design, fit\-out, mechanical and electrical coordination, and bespoke joinery for a wide range of commercial spaces. **Role Overview** This is a junior\-level opportunity for someone looking to build a long\-term career in project delivery within the fit\-out sector. The successful candidate will support live projects across Leinster, Munster, and Connacht, with approximately 60% of the role based on site, so flexibility, mobility, and a hands\-on approach will be essential. Our clients main office is based in Kildare. **Responsibilities** * Support the coordination and delivery of commercial fit\-out and interiors projects from pre\-start through to completion. * Assist with site logistics, project schedules, and progress tracking across multiple live projects. * Coordinate with subcontractors, suppliers, and internal teams to help ensure projects are progressing in line with programme. * Assist design teams throughout the project lifecycle and help coordinate key project inputs across design, M\&E, and joinery teams. * Support the management of project documentation, daily reports, resource requirements, and general site administration. * Attend sites regularly and assist with day\-to\-day coordination to help maintain quality, programme, and smooth delivery. * Liaise with project managers and the wider delivery team to ensure issues are identified and resolved quickly. * Travel to client sites across Ireland, primarily throughout Leinster, Munster, and Connacht. **Requirements** * A relevant qualification in Construction Management, Project Management, Engineering, Architectural Technology, Interior Design, or a related discipline would be preferred. * Previous experience in construction, commercial interiors, fit\-out, or a similar project\-based environment would be advantageous. * A fit\-out background would be highly desirable. * Full clean driver’s licence is essential. * Must be mobile and willing to travel regularly to sites across Ireland. * Strong organisational and coordination skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks. * Good communication skills and confidence working with internal teams, subcontractors, and suppliers. * Comfortable working in a role that combines site exposure with project coordination and administration. * Strong interest in developing a career within commercial fit\-out and project delivery. * Good IT skills, including Microsoft Office, would be beneficial. **Benefits** * Opportunity to join a growing and well\-regarded commercial interiors business delivering projects nationwide. * Excellent exposure to live fit\-out projects and strong long\-term career development potential. * Company car provided after successful completion of probation. * 3% pension contribution. * Company phone. * Laptop. *Please note that some level of Irish/UK experience is required for this role and we cannot offer out any sponsorships*

title: Junior Recruitment Consultant
description: We at SSA Group are seeking a Junior Recruitment Consultant to become part of an established team within our Dublin Office. **About Us:** SSA Group is an international recruitment business with more than 20 years in operation, partnering with leading construction and engineering companies worldwide. With offices across Ireland, the UK, North America, the Middle East, and Australia, we are a well\-established brand offering genuine opportunities to grow your career in a global environment. **The Opportunity:** We are looking to add a Junior Recruitment Consultant to our Dublin office. This is a chance to join an experienced, supportive team where you will work with an existing client base while also developing new relationships. From day one, you’ll be given a warm desk, exposure to a busy market, and ongoing support from senior management to help you build expertise and achieve success. Key responsibilities include: * Managing the full recruitment cycle, matching skilled professionals with opportunities that suit them. * Maintaining and growing relationships with clients and candidates. * Building knowledge in your market and using it to expand your desk. * Collaborating with colleagues to support the wider business. **What’s in it for you:** We offer a competitive salary, a **market\-leading commission scheme with no thresholds** , and a wide range of benefits including: * 25 days annual leave plus your birthday off * Twice\-yearly performance bonus * Flexible working options and early Friday finishes * Laptop, iPhone, and other perks depending on experience Most importantly, you’ll benefit from a culture that rewards effort, encourages career progression, and provides clear pathways to advance within the business. **What we’re looking for** * A degree in Construction, Business, HR, Marketing, or a related area. * Excellent communication skills and a professional manner. * Drive, organisation, and resilience to handle a busy workload. * A positive attitude and a strong desire to succeed in recruitment. * No prior recruitment experience is necessary – just the ambition to build a career in the sector. **For further information on this role, please contact Sean Duggan at SSA Group, or apply now.**

title: Junior Site Engineer
description: **Junior / Graduate Site Engineer – Civils \& Landscaping Project – Dublin City** A leading construction contractor is currently seeking a Junior / Graduate Site Engineer to join a major urban regeneration project in Dublin City. This is an excellent opportunity for an engineer with approximately 6 months to 1 year of site experience to gain hands\-on exposure on a high\-profile, fast\-paced city centre development. The role will be based on a large\-scale civils and hard/soft landscaping package within a significant residential\-led regeneration scheme. Works include groundworks, external finishes, public realm improvements, and associated infrastructure within a live construction environment. The successful candidate will join an established site team with strong support in place, including a Project Manager, Site Manager, and Senior Engineer. This structure provides excellent mentoring and day\-to\-day guidance, making it an ideal role for an ambitious engineer looking to progress quickly. **Key Responsibilities:** * Setting out and assisting with site engineering duties across civils and landscaping works * Supporting senior engineering staff with daily site operations * Ensuring works are carried out in line with drawings, specifications, and project requirements * Assisting with QA checks, surveys, and site records * Monitoring progress and supporting subcontractor coordination * Contributing to health \& safety compliance on a busy live site * Assisting with as\-built documentation and technical queries **Requirements:** * Degree qualified in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or related field * 6 months – 1 year site engineering experience (including placement experience considered) * Strong setting out experience or exposure * Good communication and organisational skills * Ability to work in a fast\-moving site environment * Ideally based in Dublin / commutable distance (no accommodation provided) * Preference for candidates already based locally **Salary:** €30,000 – €40,000 DOE **What’s on Offer:** * Opportunity to work on a major Dublin City regeneration scheme * Strong site support structure (PM, SM and Senior Engineer in place) * Excellent hands\-on exposure to civils and landscaping packages * Strong mentoring and development opportunities * Long\-term pipeline of projects within the contractor’s portfolio * This is a great opportunity for a motivated engineer looking to build experience on a high\-profile Dublin project with strong technical support and clear progression potential.

title: Junior UX/UI designer
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * **Shape high\-leverage product experiences.** Design exceptional UI and UX—from early concepts to polished delivery—using the best available tools to explore more directions, move faster, and raise the quality bar. Ensure every experience is intuitive, effective, and delightful. * **Start with an LLM.** Use AI tools to clarify intent, draft short specs, and surface risks, edge cases, and initial approaches before opening any design tool. Use this work to align quickly with your team and compress the time from ambiguity to direction. * **Prototype and build with AI.** Use Cursor and Claude Code to turn rough concepts into interactive prototypes and generate production\-ready frontend code. You know when to reach for code and when to reach for Figma—and you move fluidly between both to get the best result. * **Co\-create with PMs and engineers from day one.** Define UX requirements early, stay in the build through QA and release, and make engineering\-aware design decisions. * **Validate and measure.** Talk directly with customers, run quick tests, and use what you learn to adjust direction. Track funnels, retention, and adoption to assess design effectiveness and sharpen what ships next. * **Find the highest\-leverage bets from the backlog.** Identify where product improvements can unlock business value, align stakeholders around them, and move fast to deliver. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. **Commitment \& contract** Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Kubernetes Engineer
description: **Overview** We are seeking a Kubernetes Engineer to join our global infrastructure team and help scale, automate, and secure our container orchestration environments across on\-premises and public cloud platforms. As a Kubernetes specialist, you’ll work closely with DevOps, SRE, and security teams to deliver reliable, self\-service, and production\-ready Kubernetes clusters that power mission\-critical applications. **Key Responsibilities** **Cluster Management** * Deploy, manage, and upgrade Kubernetes clusters using tools like kubeadm, EKS, AKS, GKE, or Rancher. * Implement robust RBAC, network policies, ingress controllers, and security frameworks in Kubernetes. **Automation \& IaC** * Automate cluster provisioning and application deployment pipelines using Terraform, Helm, and ArgoCD. * Build reusable modules for consistent infrastructure delivery across staging and production. **CI/CD Integration** * Integrate Kubernetes with modern CI/CD workflows for rapid, safe application delivery. * Support GitOps practices and continuous deployment automation. **Monitoring, Logging \& Troubleshooting** * Implement observability for Kubernetes using Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Fluentd/Fluent Bit. * Troubleshoot performance issues, failed pods, memory leaks, and cluster degradation events. **Cloud \& Hybrid Deployments** * Operate Kubernetes workloads across AWS, Azure, GCP, and hybrid/on\-prem environments. * Use tools like Velero, Kasten, or Stash for backup/restore strategies in Kubernetes. **Collaboration \& Support** * Partner with application developers, SREs, and security teams to implement best practices. * Serve as a technical advisor on cloud\-native architectures and containerization s **Experience/Qualification Required** * Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) – Highly preferred * Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) * Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) * AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional * Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer * HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate * Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator (LFCS) **Nice to have:** * Experience with GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux). * Observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK stack. * Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd). * Open\-source contributions in Kubernetes\-related projects * Spectro cloud, Incus, Cloud 9 etc… **About InterSystems** InterSystems, a creative data technology provider, delivers a unified foundation for next\-generation applications for healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and supply chain customers in more than 80 countries. Our data platforms solve interoperability, speed, and scalability problems for large organizations around the globe to unlock the power of data and allow people to perceive data in imaginative ways. Established in 1978, InterSystems is committed to excellence through its 24×7 support for customers and partners around the world. Privately held and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, InterSystems has 38 offices in 28 countries worldwide. For more information, please visit **InterSystems.com** .

title: LDA Project Quantity Surveyor
description: **Your new company** We are currently assisting the Land Development Agency with their recruitment processes. The Land Development Agency (LDA) is the State's affordable housing delivery body. It delivers affordable homes on State\-owned or acquired land and through homebuilder partnerships as part of its Project Tosaigh initiative. The Land Development Agency's purpose is to maximise the supply of affordable homes on State and other land in a financially sustainable manner, supporting the creation of thriving communities and delivering ongoing positive social impact. Current Pipeline Snapshot: * 21,000\+ homes being assessed \& advanced across 40\+ sites (direct delivery) * 5,000\+ homes under construction (direct delivery) * 8,000\+ homes targeted delivery through the LDA Project Tosaigh initiative by 2028 * 3,000\+ people living in LDA provide affordable homes at present. The above represents the current pace of delivery to the LDA's target of delivering c. 4,000 homes per year over the coming years. These projects and homes comprise developments relating to low\-mid density housing, high\-density mixed\-use schemes and adaptive reuse of existing and heritage/protected buildings in city centre and sub\-urban locations throughout all of Ireland. **Your new role** Do you want to further develop your professional career across the full spectrum of residential property development? Do you want to join a highly motivated team of professionals working towards a common goal? Do you want to apply your core quantity surveying skills in an innovative and exciting way? This is an exciting opportunity for a dynamic quantity surveyor/cost manager with strong commercial and technical acumen to join the Cost Management Team and to support the development of the LDA's extensive development pipeline, one of the most significant development pipelines in the history of the state. The role will involve providing in\-house cost management expertise and support across multiple business functions including Strategic Planning \& Master Planning, Land Acquisition, Property Development, Construction Delivery, Developer Collaboration, Asset Management and Investment. **What to Expect** Innovation * Work in a forward\-looking environment using market leading platforms and systems to streamline QS and property development workflows. * Enjoy a collaborative and innovative culture that values fresh thinking and initiative while having opportunity to affect policy change. Sector Expert * Gain deep insight and hands\-on experience of fundamental design \& cost economics for residential development developing industry leading expertise. * Involvement in all stages of the development lifecycle \- from concept to delivery to operational management of the schemes. * Enhance experience of varying procurement models, contract and claims management across LDA portfolio involving D\&B, Employer Designed, Early Contractor Involvement, Bespoke Development Partnership agreements, Consultancy Agreements etc. * Gain key skills in detailed cost and investment modelling and viability analyses at each stage of a development's lifecycle. Network * Work alongside senior leaders of industry from all disciplines including design consultants, main contractors, developers, senior public representatives, planning, legal and sales agents offering a unique holistic view of how major developments evolve and become a reality. * Contribute and influence strategic planning and commercial decision\-making at senior level. * Regular contributions to industry and national bodies. Growth * This is not a standard QS role \- Join a rapidly growing organisation and enjoy a varied and evolving remit. You'll have the freedom to explore areas that interest you, whether that's design innovation, procurement and contract strategy, technology, modern methods of construction, site acquisition appraisals, investment modelling and sales and asset management. * Exposure to the full range of property disciplines employed by the agency with open door policy for on\-the\-job insights. * Regular training and CDP with dedicated in\-house HR Learning \& Development team. * 3rd level training subsistence available and encouraged to enhance and turbo charge career. * Guided mentorship with dedicated SCSI APC supervisor(s) and counsellor(s) to get you to the next level. **Key Responsibilities** * Visit sites in consideration and preparation of Order of Magnitude Cost Estimates and Development Budgets for early\-stage viability analysis. * Viability and cost modelling analyses. * Procurement of project design teams and construction delivery partners. * Interface and liaise closely with external consultants, building effective and collaborative working relationships. * Contributing to design team workshops offering cost, value management, procurement, contract and affordability advice. * Management of project development budget and post contract expenditure. * Liaise closely with all LDA external quantity surveying consultants. Review and assess external QS reports and cost documents. * Assisting LDA Development Managers and Construction Managers as required in the management of projects on a day\-to\-day basis. * Involvement in supply chain (Main Contractor/Developer/Consultant) negotiations where appropriate. * Maintaining and enhancing current cost databases of construction and development costs. * Ensuring development budgets and specific project gateway budget approval requests are robust. * Assist and contribute to enhancement and optimisation of LDA cost management standardised documentation, processes and function. * Assist in financial claims analyses and appraisal of construction and development soft costs. **What you'll need to succeed** * Indicative level of 2 to 8 year's demonstrable relevant experience with a Contractor, PQS or Developer. * Previous experience of residential developments preferred but not essential. * A dynamic self\-starter, ability to take ownership for assigned responsibilities and deliverables. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong interpersonal, presentation and verbal communication skills. * Be a team player with ability to work independently and collaboratively across both internal and external teams. * Microsoft Office Suite, with particular emphasis on strong MS Excel abilities. * Degree or higher in Quantity Surveying. * Chartered or willingness/in the process of gaining chartership. **What you'll get in return** In return, you will join the LDA who play an essential role in delivering some of Ireland's most critical infrastructure over the coming decades. The organisation is committed to excellence, with a strong focus on continuous learning, professional development, and long\-term career progression. You will collaborate with and lead a high\-performing team of construction, commercial, and development professionals who are passionate about delivering meaningful impact. The role offers a competitive salary, depending on experience, along with hybrid working arrangements to support work\-life balance. While primarily office\-based, the role includes site visits as required. Standard working hours are under 40 per week, Monday to Friday, with flexibility where appropriate. Additional benefits include a discretionary performance bonus, industry\-leading employer pension contributions, and significant opportunities for advancement within a purpose\-driven and rapidly growing organisation. This is more than just a job \- it is a long\-term career opportunity with real purpose and national significance. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. Hays Ireland is a trading division of Hays Specialist Recruitment (Ireland) Limited and acts as an employment agency for permanent recruitment and employment business for the supply of temporary workers. By applying for this job you accept the T\&C's, Privacy Policy and Disclaimers which can be found at hays.co.uk

title: Lecturer/Assistant Professor in Contemporary Art History, Perm
description: **Applications are invited for a permanent post of a Lecturer/Assistant Professor in Contemporary Art History within UCD School of Art History and Cultural Policy.** The successful candidate will have expertise in modern and contemporary art history, from 1950 to the present, forming a key component of the School's wide\-ranging teaching programme. Applicants must have completed a PhD in Art History or a cognate area and must have experience of teaching at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels. Candidates must have research and teaching experience beyond the field of Irish art history. Experience in global and/or non\-western art history is desirable. The successful candidate will design, deliver and assess high\-quality taught modules in the undergraduate and graduate programmes, and will undertake such administrative duties as assigned by the Head of School or their nominee. The appointee will also pursue their individual scholarly research specialism, supervise postgraduate research, and contribute to the collegial and highly engaged research culture of the School and College. UCD is a dynamic research\-intensive university that is at the forefront of research and teaching activities across a wide range of disciplines. The successful candidate will join the oldest and largest art history department on the island, with research and teaching interests that span medieval to contemporary art, and a very successful MA taught programme in Art History, Curating and Collections. **95 Lecturer/Asst Professor Above the Bar (7943\) Salary Scale: €65,401 \- €103,564 Per Annum** Appointment will be made on scale and in accordance with the Department of Finance guidelines **Closing date: 12:00 noon (local Irish time) on 05 June 2026\.** Applications must be submitted by the closing date and time specified. Any applications which are still in progress at the closing time of 12:00 noon (Local Irish Time) on the specified closing date will be cancelled automatically by the system. UCD are unable to accept late applications. UCD do not require assistance from Recruitment Agencies. Any CVs submitted by Recruitment Agencies will be returned. **Note:** Hours of work for academic staff are those as prescribed under Public Service Agreements. For further information, please follow the link: https://www.ucd.ie/hr/t4media/Academic Contract.pdf Prior to application, further information (including application procedure) should be obtained from the Work at UCD website: https://www.ucd.ie/workatucd/jobs/ **Desired Skills and Experience** Communication and interpersonal skills, Specialisation in modern and or contemporary art post 1950, Specialism in global and or non western art history, Ability and willingness to teach on broad survey modules, Willingness to work as part of a team

title: Legal Counsel - Corporate
description: At Perrigo, we are driven by our mission to ***Makes Lives Better Through Trusted Health and Wellness Solutions, Accessible to All***. We are proud to be a Top 10 player in the European Consumer Self\-Care market and the largest U.S. store brand provider of over the counter and infant formula. Dedicated to providing ***The Best Self\-Care for Everyone***, we are the people behind the brands you trust. We are Opill®, Compeed®, Solpadeine®, NiQuitin®, and many more. **We Are Perrigo**. We are committed to enhancing the wellbeing of our colleagues and consumers alike. We pride ourselves on fostering an inclusive, collaborative culture where each person can experience a sense of belonging. Join us on our One Perrigo journey as we evolve to win in self\-care. **Description Overview** ------------------------ This is an exciting opportunity to apply your legal expertise to Perrigo’s Corporate Legal team, which operates at the intersection of law and business and provides strategic legal counsel across areas such as Business Development, Corporate Governance, Corporate Finance, Tax, Treasury, and Investor Relations. As **Legal Counsel – Corporate**, you will play a crucial role in supporting the Deputy General Counsel across a broad range of high‑impact corporate matters, with **a primary focus on leading and supporting M\&A and strategic transactions**, corporate governance for a **public company headquartered in Ireland**, and complex global corporate operations. The selected candidate will be based at our Global Headquarters in **Dublin, Ireland**. While Dublin is strongly preferred, we will also consider exceptional candidates for our **London, England** office. A hybrid working schedule applies in either location. **Scope of the Role** --------------------- * M\&A: Lead and support Perrigo’s global M\&A and strategic transaction activity, including: + Managing and coordinating cross‑functional legal workstreams on acquisitions, divestitures, strategic partnerships, and other transformative transactions. + Conducting and overseeing due diligence; drafting, reviewing, and negotiating transaction agreements; and supporting carve‑out, integration planning, restructuring, and related corporate approvals. + Preparing and coordinating antitrust/competition and other regulatory filings across multiple jurisdictions. + Advising senior leadership on transaction risks, opportunities, and structuring considerations in a dynamic multinational environment. * Board \& Corporate Governance: Together with a Legal Analyst, play a critical role in supporting Perrigo’s Board of Directors and governance framework for a NYSE‑listed, Irish‑domiciled company, including: + Preparing and managing materials for quarterly Board and Committee meetings, including agendas, resolutions, briefing memos, and minutes. + Partnering with the Deputy General Counsel to maintain, update, and enhance global governance policies and governance best practices across U.S. and Irish legal frameworks. + Supporting the Annual General Meeting (AGM), including planning, drafting AGM materials, managing shareholder and regulatory requirements in Ireland, and ensuring compliance with Irish and U.S. governance norms. + Leading quarterly governance updates, monitoring regulatory and stewardship‑related developments (e.g. ISS, Glass Lewis, Irish law, NYSE), and presenting trends to senior leadership. + Developing and delivering training modules and education materials for Directors, including Ireland\-specific governance dynamics and cross‑jurisdictional fiduciary duties. * Subsidiary Entity Management: Together with the Corporate Governance Analyst, serve as a senior legal advisor on Perrigo’s global corporate matters, including: + Overseeing global entity management, board/corporate secretarial functions, and corporate recordkeeping across Europe, North America, and other regions. + Supporting subsidiary governance enhancements and internal corporate simplification efforts. * Corporate Finance, Disclosure and Securities Regulation: Provide targeted, strategic support on U.S. securities law matters, including: + Assisting with periodic reporting and disclosure considerations. + Reviewing press releases, earnings materials, and investor communications for legal and risk considerations. + Advising on insider trading, Section 16, and corporate disclosure controls as needed. * Tax \& Treasury: Provide targeted, strategic support on tax \& treasury matters, including financing activities; M\&A\-related tax structuring; and domestic and international tax matters. * General Corporate Matters: Provide legal support on a broad range of general corporate matters, including entity management, corporate compliance, and other day\-to\-day corporate legal issues impacting the company’s operations. Serve as a cross functional strategic partner to Finance, Tax, and Global Business Development. **Experience Required** ----------------------- * A qualified and licensed attorney; admission to practice law in Ireland or England. * Typically 6\-10\+ years of legal experience working with international publicly traded companies, preferably with a combination of law firm and in\-house experience. * The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of law firm M\&A experience and adjacent corporate practice areas. * Demonstrated ability to manage senior executive relations and ability to collaborate and build relations with stakeholders across functional and organizational lines. * Strong understanding of relevant laws and regulations governing the industry, combined with an ability to explain complex legal concepts in simple, actionable terms to stakeholders located in different jurisdictions. * Strong strategic thinking and business acumen, with the ability to balance legal considerations with business objectives. * Exceptional leadership, interpersonal and communication skills, including well\-developed interpersonal skills and strong written communication skills. * Discretion \& Integrity – the ability to handle sensitive information with the highest level of confidentiality and ethics. * Project management skills – the ability to manage multiple legal matters and deadlines. Familiarity with legal tech tools is also a bonus. **Benefits** We believe our people are our greatest asset. Alongside competitive compensation, we offer benefits tailored to supporting you and your family, as well as career development opportunities to ensure you feel valued and supported, both professionally and personally. Find out more about **Total Rewards** at Perrigo. **Hybrid Working Approach** We love our offices and the setting they provide for in\-person collaboration and celebration. But we also appreciate the opportunity to work remotely can energise you too, so we promote flexibility with the ability to work two days a week from home in many roles. We are proud to be included in the Forbes list of "America's Best Employers by State 2024". Find out more **here** **Applicants please note:** To apply to this position please click the APPLY button at the bottom of the application. (The SAVE button will only save your profile information but not submit an application for this open position.) Thank you. *We are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or other characteristics protected by law. \#weareperrigo*

title: Level 2 IT Support Engineer
description: My Dublin City Centre based client is recruiting for an IT Support Engineer (Level 2\) to join a growing team. This is a permanent role and will be fully onsite. The IT Support Engineer will support and maintain the firm’s Microsoft\-based IT environment. The role involves providing advanced technical support across Microsoft 365, Azure, desktop systems, networking, and end\-user services in a fast\-paced professional environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide Level 2 support for desktops, laptops, mobile devices, and remote users. * Support Microsoft 365 services including Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Exchange Online. * Administer Azure / Entra ID, Intune, MFA, and user access management. * Troubleshoot Windows 10/11, networking, VPN, printer, and hardware issues. * Assist with server support, Active Directory, patching, and backups. * Support cybersecurity, endpoint protection, and compliance standards. * Participate in IT projects, upgrades, and continuous improvement initiatives. **Required Skills** * Experience supporting Microsoft 365 and Azure environments. * Strong knowledge of Windows desktop support and troubleshooting. * Experience with Intune / Endpoint Manager and Entra ID. * Understanding of networking fundamentals (DNS, DHCP, VPN, TCP/IP). * Excellent communication and customer service skills. * Experience in a legal or professional services environment desirable. **Qualifications** * IT qualification or equivalent experience. * Microsoft certifications advantageous.

description: **Job Description** **SHIFTS YOU ARE APPLYING FOR : Rotational Shifts** **The Role:** Working within our Loss Prevention team at NEXT within our Blanchardstown store you’ll effectively support the provision of a safe and secure working and shopping environment, providing a uniformed presence to protect the company assets and to minimise loss. **What You'll Take On:** * Ensure our stores feel safe and secure places to work and shop. * Work closely with store teams and build strong relationships with local police. * Identify any high\-risk areas and act as a strong deterrent on the shop floor. * Engage with store management to ensure company LP standards within stores are adhered to at all times. * Action any other duties as requested by the Regional LP Manager including out of hours surveillance, LP auditing and additional support at other stores and attend department meetings. **What You'll Bring:** * A valid PSA licence * Previous security experience within a retail environment is preferred. * Flexibility and willingness to work across different stores and opening times. * Able to travel on a day to day basis to cover a cluster of stores within a reasonable travelling distance from your home address. * A commitment to keeping our store teams and customers safe * Well organised with the ability to work well in a fast and busy store. * Hardworking, self motivated and always ready to use your initiative. * A confident communicator who can build great relationships with store teams, police and customers. * Fully flexible to work 5 out of 7 days including weekends and evenings. * Comfortable with using IT systems/computers is preferred but not essential. **About Us** You know Next, but did you know we’re a FTSE\-100 retail company employing over 44,000 people across the Next group. We’re the UK’s 2nd largest fashion retailer and for Kidswear we’re the market leader. At the last count we have over 450 stores, plus the Next Online and it’s now possible to buy on\-line from over 80 countries around the world! So we’ve gone global! **About The Team** * 25% off most NEXT, MADE\*, Lipsy\*, Gap\* and Victoria's Secret\* products (\*when purchased through NEXT) * Company performance based bonus * Sharesave scheme * 10% off most partner brands \& up to 15% off Branded Beauty * Early VIP access to sale stock * Access a digital GP and other free health and wellbeing services * Financial Wellbeing \- Save, track and enhance your financial wellbeing * Apprenticeship \- Grow and develop on the job whilst gaining a qualification * Direct to Work \- Discount online and instore, collect your items the next day for free from your place of work or local store * Support Networks \- Access to Network Groups to empower and celebrate each other * Wellhub \- Discounted flexible monthly gym memberships, with apps, PT sessions and more Conditions apply to all benefits. These benefits are discretionary and subject to change. We aim to support all candidates during the application process and are happy to provide workplace adjustments when necessary. Should you need support with your application due to a disability or long\-term condition, feel free to get in touch with us by email headoffice\_careers@next.co.uk (please include 'Workplace Adjustments' in the subject line), or call us on 0116 284 2486 and leave a voicemail.

title: Marketing Coordinator
description: **About MarketStar:** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. **About The Marketing Coordinator:** We are looking for a B2B marketing and operations professional who understands both how messaging is built and how it performs in the field. This is not a pure content role — it is a hybrid of marketing operations, sales enablement, and regional adaptation, where you will take globally developed messaging frameworks and translate them into market\-relevant, high\-performing sales plays for EMEA teams. If you are someone who enjoys working at the intersection of strategy, execution, and field impact, this role will be a strong fit. **Location:** Hybrid, Leopardstown, Dublin 18 (Tuesday, Wednesday \& Thursday in office) **What will you do?** * Adapt and localize globally approved email templates and sales cadences to align with EMEA\-specific industries, personas, and market nuances. * Serve as the primary liaison between central strategy teams and EMEA sales teams, ensuring alignment with regional priorities. * Embed sales plays into rep workflows through structured rollout, enablement sessions, and field engagement. * Align outreach with regional campaigns, pipeline priorities, and buyer context. * Capture and synthesize feedback from sales teams to identify adoption gaps and messaging improvements. * Monitor adoption trends and execution quality across regions, surfacing insights to central teams. * Ensure consistency between global frameworks and local execution while maintaining design principles and guardrails. * Create content that aligns with the buyer journey, motivating action across digital platforms. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to align messaging with strategic priorities. * Conduct in\-depth research to deliver industry\-specific narratives and insights. * Track content performance and refine strategies based on key metrics **Skills \& Qualifications** * Proven experience in creating B2B marketing content across campaigns, with exposure to SaaS or tech industries being a plus. * Experience in marketing operations, or sales enablement, preferably in SaaS or enterprise technology environments. * Strong understanding of email campaigns, sequencing, and demand generation workflows. * Experience working with CRM and sales engagement platforms (e.g., Salesforce, Salesloft). * Ability to adapt messaging for multi\-geo campaigns, with sensitivity to regional nuances. * Strong collaboration skills with experience working across sales, marketing, and operations teams. * Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret performance signals and translate them into actionable insights **What we offer?** Through commitment to our “We Care” value, we believe in providing employees with valuable mental and physical well\-being benefits including: * Comprehensive VHI Health cover from day one * Company Pension Scheme * 25 Days Annual Leave * Structured training \& career development opportunities * Education reimbursement * Paid birthday leave * Personal Hardship Loan Program * Mental health and 24/7 employee
